evidence.claim: ContextEngine is a fully pluggable interface with token-budget assembly, background/foreground maintenance, transcript rewrite API, and subagent spawn hooks
evidence.workrail_relevance: HIGH -- WorkTrain has no pluggable context management. Context injection is hardcoded in buildSystemPrompt().

evidence.claim: Prompt cache observability: ContextEnginePromptCacheInfo tracks retention, lastCallUsage, observation (broke/changes), lastCacheTouchAt, expiresAt
evidence.workrail_relevance: MEDIUM -- WorkTrain has no prompt cache tracking. Sessions could hit expensive context rebuilds silently.

evidence.claim: TaskRegistry: SQLite-backed, status union (queued/running/succeeded/failed/timed_out/cancelled/lost), TaskDeliveryStatus, TaskNotifyPolicy (done_only/state_changes/silent), parentFlowId tree structure
evidence.workrail_relevance: HIGH -- WorkTrain has no persistent task registry. Session outcomes live in execution-stats.jsonl (append-only, not queryable).

evidence.claim: Commitment extraction: LLM extracts promises from conversation, stores with due window, timezone, dedup key, confidence score, heartbeat delivery
evidence.workrail_relevance: MEDIUM -- WorkTrain has report_issue but no mechanism to extract/track agent-made commitments (e.g. 'I will open a PR'). This is the operator-feedback-memory gap.

evidence.claim: Subagent roles: main/orchestrator/leaf typed roles, control scope (children/none), spawn depth persisted to session store and resolved via spawnedBy chain
evidence.workrail_relevance: MEDIUM -- WorkTrain has depth enforcement but no role model. All sessions are 'leaf' in practice. No orchestrator role distinguishes coordinator sessions from worker sessions.

evidence.claim: ACP (Agent Control Protocol): session-mode and run-mode inter-agent communication, parent stream relay, persistent bindings
evidence.workrail_relevance: LOW-MEDIUM -- WorkTrain uses signal_coordinator for mid-session coordinator signals. ACP is more general-purpose inter-agent comms.

evidence.claim: Trajectory trace schema: unified TrajectoryEvent (traceId, source=runtime|transcript|export, type, ts, seq, sessionId, runId, workspaceDir, provider, modelId)
evidence.workrail_relevance: HIGH -- WorkTrain has two separate event systems (daemon event log ~/.workrail/events/ + v2 session event store). No unified trace schema. Console correlation requires bridging two formats.

evidence.claim: Service audit: typed issue codes (gatewayCommandMissing, gatewayPathMissing, gatewayTokenEmbedded, launchdKeepAlive, systemdAfterNetworkOnline, etc.), ServiceConfigAudit {ok, issues[]}
evidence.workrail_relevance: HIGH -- WorkTrain has no service audit command. worktrain daemon --start reports success even when daemon crashes immediately (known backlog item). No typed issue codes for config problems.

evidence.claim: Detached launchd restart handoff: spawns /bin/sh child that waits for PID exit then kickstarts service. mode=kickstart or start-after-exit. Undo any launchctl disable before restart.
evidence.workrail_relevance: MEDIUM -- WorkTrain does not have a clean restart handoff mechanism. Binary update + restart has a gap where the daemon is down.

evidence.claim: Doctor --fix: auto-repairs typed service config issues (bad cron model, token drift, stale binary paths, node runtime migration)
evidence.workrail_relevance: HIGH -- WorkTrain has no doctor command. worktrain daemon --start silently fails with old binary (known backlog bug).

evidence.claim: OpenClaw explicitly rejects agent-hierarchy frameworks (manager-of-managers) in VISION.md
evidence.workrail_relevance: STRATEGIC -- WorkTrain's coordinator pipeline IS a hierarchy framework. OpenClaw is philosophically opposed. Different design philosophies, not convergence.

evidence.claim: Plugin/extension architecture: core stays lean, capabilities go to ClawHub (external registry). Bundle-style plugins preferred over code plugins.
evidence.workrail_relevance: MEDIUM -- WorkTrain workflows are the plugin surface. No external registry equivalent yet. Could adopt bundle-style 'workflow package' distribution.

evidence.claim: Windows (WSL2/schtasks) + Linux (systemd) support alongside macOS (launchd)
evidence.workrail_relevance: LOW -- WorkTrain is macOS-only today. Cross-platform is not a current WorkTrain priority.

evidence.claim: Memory: pluggable slot (one active at a time), multiple options today, converging on one default
evidence.workrail_relevance: MEDIUM -- WorkTrain has no persistent memory mechanism. Operator preference memory is a known backlog gap.

evidence.claim: Cron jobs lost after restart because activeJobIds not persisted (bug)
evidence.workrail_relevance: LOW -- WorkTrain uses session sidecars for crash recovery. Relevant as a reminder that ephemeral job state must be persisted.

evidence.claim: Context assembly cache invalidation bug: stale history reused after reset/assembly failure
evidence.workrail_relevance: LOW -- Confirms that context assembly bugs are common and hard. WorkTrain should test cache invalidation paths.

evidence.claim: Sponsor list: OpenAI, GitHub, NVIDIA, Vercel, Blacksmith, Convex
evidence.workrail_relevance: NONE -- competitive intelligence only

evidence.claim: release cadence: daily CalVer (v2026.5.x), 35k commits in 90 days, 13/13 active weeks
evidence.workrail_relevance: LOW -- velocity signal only. WorkTrain releases are semantic-version-driven, not daily.

evidence.claim: Workspace inheritance on spawn: explicit > target-agent workspace > requester workspace
evidence.workrail_relevance: MEDIUM -- WorkTrain spawn_agent always uses caller-provided workspacePath. No automatic workspace inheritance from parent or target agent.

evidence.claim: LLM-assisted exec approvals: feature request to use LLM reasoning for tool approval policy
evidence.workrail_relevance: NONE -- not WorkTrain's domain

gaps.description: User sentiment on context quality -- do users praise/complain about context injection depth? No G2/Reddit checked.
gaps.note: Would require G2 or Reddit search. Not available in this session. Marked decision_critical=false because source code evidence is sufficient for roadmap_prioritization.

gaps.description: ClawHub monetization model -- is it paid? Revenue structure unknown.
gaps.note: Could check clawhub.ai. Not relevant to WorkTrain roadmap.

gaps.description: Memory plugin implementations -- what algorithms do the current memory options use? (src/memory/root-memory-files.ts is the only file there)
gaps.note: Memory plugins likely live in extensions/. Could check. Low priority for WorkTrain comparison.

gaps.description: How does OpenClaw handle session compaction in practice -- what algorithm does the default context engine use?
gaps.note: ContextEngine interface is clear; the default implementation is in a plugin. Could read. Interesting but not blocking.
